content:
# Final Review Arbiter
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Rejected amendment fr-17-dde66e40 (RunResumed fallback). While the amendment correctly identifies that try_reconcile_success only searches for RunStarted events, its proposed remedies (comment or RunResumed fallback) are insufficient. The real danger is not a missing RunStarted but a stale one — reopened projects retain the original RunStarted while adding a newer RunResumed, so fallback-on-missing never triggers. The correct fix is fr-17-2f3210bd (persisting run_id on DaemonTask), which all parties accepted and which renders this amendment's changes moot.

## Rulings

- `fr-17-dde66e40`: Reject
  Reviewer 1's rejection argument is decisive. The amendment proposes two options: (a) add a comment documenting the append-only invariant, or (b) add RunResumed as a fallback when RunStarted is not found. Neither option addresses the actual correctness problem. As Reviewer 1 correctly identifies, when a project is reopened, the original RunStarted event remains in the journal (it's append-only), so the fallback-on-missing-RunStarted logic would never trigger — the code would still pick the original (wrong) RunStarted rather than the newer RunResumed. Adding a comment documenting an invariant that doesn't actually guarantee correctness in the reopen scenario is misleading. Furthermore, all parties unanimously accepted fr-17-2f3210bd, which is the proper fix: persisting the workflow run_id on DaemonTask eliminates journal scanning entirely. Once that fix lands, any comment or fallback added by this amendment becomes dead code or a stale comment on removed logic. Accepting this amendment would either (1) add insufficient hardening that gives false confidence, or (2) add code/comments immediately superseded by the correct fix in fr-17-2f3210bd. Reject to avoid wasted effort and misleading documentation.
